human:
POINTS:
- [build.gradle:802] @gharris1727  without this ignoreFailures extension, check fails for almost any module I see.
v5:
SUMMARY:
This PR introduces the de.thetaphi.forbiddenapis Gradle plugin to detect usage of forbidden JDK APIs (starting with the jdk-unsafe bundled signature). The check is added to all subprojects with ignoreFailures=true for now. The initial approach is reasonable, but there is a significant coverage gap for the :core project.

POINTS:
- [build.gradle:~800] **The `:core` project will be silently excluded from forbidden API checks.** The `:core` project configures `sourceSets { main { java { srcDirs = [] } } }` (lines 1334-1352), routing all sources through the Scala compiler. The forbiddenapis plugin resolves sources from the Java source set, so `forbiddenApisMain` will have ZERO sources to analyze for `:core` — despite 19 Java files existing in `core/src/main/java/`. This creates a coverage gap. Consider configuring the `:core` project explicitly to include its Java sources for forbiddenapis, or document this limitation.

- [build.gradle:800] **`ignoreFailures = true` makes the check effectively non-enforcing.** The check will report violations but never fail the build. This is fine for a discovery phase, but consider adding a follow-up ticket/JIRA to enable enforcement once known violations are addressed, or document this explicitly in the PR description.

- [build.gradle:803] **Test sources are not checked.** The dependency adds only `forbiddenApisMain` to the test task, but the existing checkstyle dependency includes both `checkstyleMain` and `checkstyleTest`. For consistency with how checkstyle is integrated, consider also adding `forbiddenApisTest` to catch forbidden API usage in test code.

- [build.gradle:797-802] **The plugin is applied to ALL subprojects unconditionally.** This includes organizational-only projects like `:connect` (which is just a directory with no code) and `:generator`. While not harmful, consider adding a condition to skip subprojects that have no Java sources, or document that the plugin is applied broadly.

- [build.gradle:800] **Only `jdk-unsafe` bundled signature is included.** This is a reasonable starting point per the PR description ("Part of signatures"). Consider planning to add more signatures later (e.g., `jdk-system-out`, `jdk-non-portable`, `commons-io-unsafe`) to broaden coverage incrementally.
v7:
SUMMARY:
This PR adds the forbiddenapis Gradle plugin to check for forbidden API usage across Kafka subprojects, initially with `ignoreFailures = true` and only the `jdk-unsafe` signature. The approach is generally consistent with existing quality tool patterns in the build.

POINTS:
- [build.gradle:46] Plugin declared without `apply false` — inconsistent with other plugins (spotbugs, scoverage, shadow all use `apply false` at root level then `apply plugin: '...'` inside subprojects). Since the root project has no Java sources (confirmed: no `src/main/java` at root, all sources in subprojects), this is functionally harmless but breaks the established convention. Consider adding `apply false` for consistency.

- [build.gradle:801] Only `forbiddenApisMain` is added to `test.dependsOn()`, but `forbiddenApisTest` is not. Checkstyle runs on both main AND test sources (`checkstyleMain`, `checkstyleTest`), so for completeness, should forbiddenapis also scan test sources? If intentional, add a comment explaining why test sources are excluded.

- [build.gradle:799-801] The `forbiddenApis` configuration block sets `ignoreFailures = true` at the extension level, which applies to both main and test tasks. This is fine for initial adoption, but be aware that when `ignoreFailures` is later set to `false`, it will affect both tasks if/when `forbiddenApisTest` is also added.

- [build.gradle:804] The `forbiddenApisMain` and `forbiddenApisTest` tasks are not configured with `group = 'Verification'` like checkstyle tasks (see build.gradle lines 792-797). This makes the tasks less discoverable via `./gradlew tasks`. Consider adding: `tasks.withType(de.thetaphi.forbiddenapis.gradle.ForbiddenApisTask).configureEach { group = 'Verification' }`.

- [build.gradle:856] The trailing newline at end of file is removed (`\ No newline at end of file`). This is a minor style regression — the original file had a trailing newline.

- [build.gradle:799] The `jdk-unsafe` bundled signature checks for Locale constructors, Charset.forName(), TimeZone.getDefault(), etc. — good starting point. Note that the `jdk-system-out` signature (which flags `System.out`/`System.err` usage) may be worth considering in a follow-up since Kafka uses SLF4J for logging.

- No suppression file pattern is established (unlike spotbugs which uses `gradle/spotbugs-exclude.xml`). Consider creating a suppression file early if you anticipate needing to suppress findings, rather than relying solely on `ignoreFailures = true`.
